name: hig-inputs
description: Apple Human Interface Guidelines for input methods and interaction patterns across iOS, iPadOS, macOS, watchOS, tvOS, and visionOS platforms.
capabilities: Provide official Apple design guidance for touch gestures and interactions
Recommend keyboard shortcuts and navigation patterns for iOS, iPadOS, and macOS
Explain Apple Pencil features including Scribble, pressure sensitivity, and hover interactions
Guide game controller integration with MFi standards and on-screen fallbacks
Detail spatial input patterns for visionOS including eye tracking and hand gestures
Specify focus system requirements for tvOS and visionOS navigation
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis detected 905 potential security issues across 13 files (1673 lines). All findings are false positives from legitimate documentation content. Markdown backtick syntax for code symbols was misidentified as shell execution. HTTP URLs are legitimate Apple Developer Documentation links. C2 keyword detections are normal UI terminology (control, command, etc.). No actual security risks found.
valueStatement: Apple platform input methods require platform-specific design patterns. This skill provides official Human Interface Guidelines for gestures, keyboards, controllers, and spatial interactions across all Apple platforms.
limitations: Content focuses on Apple platforms only, does not cover Android or web input patterns
Guidelines are design recommendations, not implementation code or API references
Some advanced topics like ARKit spatial gestures require cross-referencing other skills
Platform-specific constraints may require additional verification against current Apple documentation

useCases.title: Design gesture shortcuts for iOS app
useCases.description: iOS developer needs to add custom swipe gestures to photo editing app. Use this skill to learn standard gesture patterns, avoid conflicts with system gestures, and implement proper recognizer configurations.

useCases.title: Add keyboard shortcuts to iPadOS app
useCases.description: Productivity app developer adding Command key shortcuts. Use this skill to understand standard shortcut conventions, implement full keyboard navigation, and make shortcuts discoverable in the Command key overlay.

useCases.title: Implement Apple Pencil drawing features
useCases.description: Note-taking app needs Apple Pencil support. Use this skill to learn about pressure sensitivity, tilt, hover interactions, Scribble text entry, and distinguishing between Pencil and finger input.

promptTemplates.title: Basic gesture guidance
promptTemplates.prompt: What standard gestures should my iOS app support?
promptTemplates.scenario: Developer starting iOS app design needs fundamental gesture patterns

promptTemplates.title: Apple Pencil implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I add Apple Pencil support to my drawing app? What sensors and interactions should I implement?
promptTemplates.scenario: Developer building creative app needs stylus input guidance

promptTemplates.title: Keyboard shortcut design
promptTemplates.prompt: What keyboard shortcuts should I provide for my productivity app? How do they appear in the Command key overlay?
promptTemplates.scenario: Developer optimizing iPadOS app for keyboard workflows

promptTemplates.title: visionOS spatial input planning
promptTemplates.prompt: What input methods and interaction patterns should I design for in my visionOS immersive experience?
promptTemplates.scenario: Developer building spatial computing app needs eye tracking and gesture guidance

bestPractices: Support multiple input methods simultaneously - design for touch, pointer, keyboard, and Pencil together rather than in isolation
Use built-in gesture recognizers and platform APIs instead of implementing custom gesture handlers - this ensures accessibility support and consistent edge case handling
Provide consistent visual and haptic feedback for every input action - users should always know their interaction was registered
antiPatterns: Override system gestures like edge swipes for notifications or Home screen - this creates conflicting interactions and frustrates users
Require mode switches before Apple Pencil can draw - users expect immediate marking when Pencil touches screen
Hide keyboard shortcuts from the Command key overlay - undiscoverable shortcuts reduce productivity for power users

faq.question: Should I support Apple Pencil or touch input?
faq.answer: Support both. Let users choose when to switch between Apple Pencil and finger input. Ensure controls respond to both input types so users do not need to switch modes to activate UI elements.

faq.question: What is the difference between focus and hover on visionOS?
faq.answer: Focus indicates keyboard or controller navigation target on tvOS and visionOS. Hover is the visionOS-specific eye tracking effect that shows content will respond when user looks at it. They serve different purposes and can appear independently.

faq.question: Do I need to support game controllers?
faq.answer: Game controller support is optional but recommended for games on iOS, tvOS, and Mac. Map to extended gamepad profile with sensible defaults. Always provide touch or keyboard alternatives for non-game apps.

faq.question: How do I handle keyboard avoidance?
faq.answer: Use the keyboard avoidance APIs to adjust your layout when the software keyboard appears. Never cover essential content with the keyboard. Move or resize the active text field into view before input begins.

faq.question: What are the standard gestures I must support?
faq.answer: Standard gestures include tap to activate, swipe to scroll and navigate, pinch to zoom, long press for context menus, and drag to move. Never override system edge swipes for Home, back, notifications, or app switching.

faq.question: Can I create custom gestures?
faq.answer: Yes, but prefer using standard recognizers. If you create custom gestures, make them discoverable with hints or coaching. Avoid requiring multiple simultaneous interactions as these are hard to discover and inaccessible.
